description: This figure shows four MatCalc-simulated Time-Temperature-Precipitation (TTP) diagrams for investigated low-density nano-bainitic Al-C steels: subplot (a) for steel S1 (approximately representative for S2), (b) for S3, (c) for S4, (d) for S5. All diagrams use log-scaled time (s) as x-axis and temperature (°C) as y-axis. Curves in each diagram mark 5%, 50%, 95% precipitation fractions of (Fe,M)ₓCᵧ carbides, and subplot (c) additionally marks the precipitation region of TiMoC complex carbides. Each subplot labels the austenitization temperature Tₐ, a uniform cooling rate of 100 K s⁻¹, and austempering temperature Tᵦ with corresponding holding time Bt. Comparisons show that (Fe,M)ₓCᵧ precipitation kinetics is extremely fast between ~700 °C and 1150 °C in S1/S3, and 700-1000 °C in S5, making their avoidance impossible. For S4, initial (Fe,M)ₓCᵧ nucleation between 700-950 °C is followed by suppression from the counter-coarsening effect of TiMoC precipitates. S3 shows slightly slower (Fe,M)ₓCᵧ precipitation kinetics than S1, while S5 has a narrower precipitation temperature range. These TTP diagrams reveal the regulation effect of alloying elements (Al, Ti, Mo) on precipitation kinetics, supporting optimization of heat treatment processes for the studied steels.
